title: hi explain the architecture as how openclaw is integrated in

messages.content: hi explain the architecture as how openclaw is integrated in which files.

OpenClaw is integrated through the LLM/provider layer, then selected by runtime orchestration.


Main execution entry


runtime/agentChain.js → answerCustomerViaConfiguredMode(...)


Reads backend config via getFlowConfig("customer") from ../providers/llm


Builds context (history, DB schema/context, notes, RAG hints), then runs the configured backend path.




Likely OpenClaw execution files


runtime/customerBackendPipeline → backend options + response transform


runtime/customerBackendEnsemble → multi-backend / ensemble execution


providers/llm → actual OpenClaw/provider binding




Routing / orchestration before OpenClaw


runtime/customerFlow.js


Handles customer turn lifecycle


Uses routeCustomerMessage(...)


Calls decideCustomerExecution(...) / getCustomerExecutionConfig(...)


Applies policy + state before backend execution






Workspace-scoped config


core/workspace.js → active workspace


setup/profileService.js → workspace profile / DB path




Bottom line


customerFlow.js decides


agentChain.js prepares


providers/llm + backend pipeline/ensemble actually integrate OpenClaw

check which model is configured for openclaw as of now

Current OpenClaw model is not hardcoded here; it is loaded from config/settings.json.


How it’s selected


providers/llm.js → getFlowConfig("customer" | "admin" | "agent")


Reads settings.flows[flow].llm


If backend !== "direct", provider becomes backend adapter:


providerKey = (cfg.backend && cfg.backend !== "direct") ? "backend" : (cfg.provider || "openai")






So “OpenClaw” = backend mode, not a named provider in this file.


Where current model should be


config/settings.json


likely under one of:


flows.customer.llm.model


flows.admin.llm.model


flows.agent.llm.model


or fallback legacy fields






Routing behavior


runtime/flowOrchestrator.js only decides tool vs backend, not which model.




Answer


Configured OpenClaw model cannot be named from these files alone.


The source of truth is config/settings.json.
